Marmidon Enterprise Software - Corporate Website Revamp

_The single source of truth for the Marmidon website. Defines why we are building, who we are building for, what the site must do, and how we will judge it complete._

| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Programme**      | Marmidon Corporate Website Revamp 2026                         |
| **Target domain**  | <www.marmidon.com> (replacing marmidon.kimdigitary.com)        |

| **Status**         | Approved for build                                             |
| **Classification** | Confidential - Vendor & Stakeholder                            |
| **Audience**       | Website developer, designer, content team, project sponsor     |

**Marmidon Website Programme - Business Analysis Workstream**

Senior Business Analyst (Corporate & Enterprise Web)

_CONFIDENTIAL - For the Marmidon website development team and authorised stakeholders only._

# **Document Control**

### **Version history**

| **Ver.** | **Date** | **Author**    | **Summary of change**             | **Status**   |
| -------- | -------- | ------------- | --------------------------------- | ------------ |
| 0.1      | 2026-06  | BA Workstream | Initial skeleton and benchmarking | Draft        |
| 0.7      | 2026-06  | BA Workstream | Functional requirements baselined | Review       |
| **1.0**  | 2026-06  | BA Workstream | Issued for development handover   | **Approved** |

### **Reviewers & approvals**

| **Role**                   | **Responsibility**                    | **Approval** |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ------------ |
| Project Sponsor (Marmidon) | Business objectives, budget, sign-off | Required     |
| Product Owner              | Scope, priorities, acceptance         | Required     |
| Senior Business Analyst    | Requirements integrity & traceability | Author       |
| Lead Web Developer         | Technical feasibility confirmation    | Required     |
| Brand / Marketing Lead     | Messaging, tone, visual direction     | Required     |

### **Companion documents in this suite**

This PRD is the parent document. The following companion deliverables expand specific areas and must remain consistent with it. Where a conflict is found, this PRD prevails until formally amended.

| **Code**    | **Document**                             | **Purpose**                                                   |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **PRD-001** | This document                            | Why / who / what / acceptance - source of truth               |
| WDD-002     | Website Design Document                  | Brand system, UI components, page-by-page layout & wireframes |
| SIA-003     | Site Map & Information Architecture      | Page hierarchy, navigation, URL & redirect map                |
| TSR-004     | Technical, SEO & Analytics Specification | Stack, hosting, performance, security, SEO, tracking          |
| CPM-005     | Content Plan & Page-Content Matrix       | Per-page content blocks, copy guidance, asset list            |

**Table of Contents**

# **1\. Executive Summary**

_Marmidon is replacing a placeholder website with a credible, conversion-focused corporate presence worthy of an enterprise ERP vendor._

Marmidon is a software company that develops, licenses, supports and maintains the **Marmidon Enterprise Software System** - an integrated ERP platform spanning finance, human resources, inventory, procurement, sales, operations and fleet, sold across twelve industry sectors. The current site, _marmidon.kimdigitary.com_, is a placeholder that does not reflect the depth of the product or build buyer confidence.

This programme delivers a new website at **<www.marmidon.com>** that does four jobs at once: it explains a complex product clearly, it earns trust through proof, it is found in search and by AI answer engines, and it converts qualified visitors into demo requests. Nearly nine in ten B2B buyers review multiple vendor websites before they ever speak to sales - the website is therefore the primary sales instrument, not a brochure.

**BEST PRACTICE · The conversion job to be done**

Primary goal: a qualified visitor requests a demo or contacts sales.

Secondary goal: a not-yet-ready visitor downloads a resource or subscribes, entering nurture.

Every page must make the next logical step obvious. A site that cannot answer "if you wanted X, where would you click?" in one guess has failed its architecture test.

This document benchmarks how leading ERP vendors structure their sites, defines the audiences and their journeys, specifies every functional requirement with a unique identifier and priority, and states the acceptance criteria against which the developer's work will be accepted.

### **Headline targets**

| **12**<br><br>**INDUSTRY SECTORS** | **11**<br><br>**ERP MODULES** | **≤2.5s**<br><br>**LCP TARGET** | **WCAG 2.2 AA**<br><br>**ACCESSIBILITY** |
| ---------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |

**SECTION 2**

**Business Context & Background**

Who Marmidon is, the product, the problem and the opportunity

# **2\. Business Context & Background**

## **2.1 About Marmidon**

Marmidon is a software company that develops, licenses, supports, maintains and offers related commercial services around the Marmidon Enterprise Software System, an enterprise-grade ERP platform. Marmidon's value proposition is a single, integrated system of record that unifies the core operational and financial functions of a mid-sized to large organisation, removing the cost and risk of stitching together disconnected point solutions and spreadsheets.

## **2.2 The product at a glance**

The Marmidon Enterprise Software System is a modular ERP suite. Buyers can begin with the modules they need and extend over time, with all modules sharing one data model so that information entered once is available everywhere. The website must communicate both the breadth of the suite and the depth of each module.

### **Module suite (the canonical list used across all website documents)**

| **#** | **Module**                             | **What the website must convey it does**                                                                    |
| ----- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| M1    | **Financial Management & Accounting**  | General ledger, payables, receivables, multi-currency, budgeting, financial reporting, statutory compliance |
| M2    | **Human Resources & Payroll**          | Employee records, payroll, leave, appraisal, statutory deductions, HR self-service                          |
| M3    | **Inventory & Warehouse**              | Stock control, multi-location, barcoding, valuation, reorder, stock takes                                   |
| M4    | **Procurement & Suppliers**            | Requisitions, purchase orders, supplier management, approvals, goods receipt                                |
| M5    | **Sales & CRM**                        | Quotations, orders, invoicing, customer management, pipeline, receivables link                              |
| M6    | **Operations Management**              | Service/operations workflows, job tracking, scheduling, resource allocation                                 |
| M7    | **Fleet Management**                   | Vehicle register, fuel, maintenance, assignment, cost tracking                                              |
| M8    | **Manufacturing & Production**         | Bills of material, work orders, production planning, costing, shop-floor                                    |
| M9    | **Point of Sale & Retail**             | POS, retail pricing, promotions, multi-outlet, daily reconciliation                                         |
| M10   | **Business Intelligence & Dashboards** | KPI dashboards, analytics, configurable reports, data export                                                |
| M11   | **Project & Job Costing**              | Project budgets, time & cost capture, profitability, billing                                                |

**NOTE · Why this list matters**

This eleven-module list is the master taxonomy. It drives the Products mega-menu (SIA-003), the module landing pages (WDD-002), and the content matrix (CPM-005). If a module is added, removed or renamed, it must be changed here first and cascaded to every companion document.

## **2.3 Sectors served**

Marmidon Enterprise Software is sold across twelve industry sectors. Each sector needs its own solution page that re-frames the product in the buyer's own language and pain points - manufacturers do not search the way hospitals do.

**Manufacturing** **Production** **Wholesale** **Retail** **Distribution** **Professional Services (IT, Legal)** **Education** **Healthcare** **Hospitality** **Non-Profit** **Construction** **Media & Publishing**

## **2.4 The problem with the current site**

| **Problem with marmidon.kimdigitary.com**             | **Business impact**                                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Placeholder presence on a sub-domain of a third party | Signals an unestablished vendor; erodes enterprise credibility before evaluation begins |
| No articulation of product depth or modules           | Buyers cannot self-qualify; high-intent traffic bounces                                 |
| No proof (cases, logos, testimonials)                 | Fails the trust test B2B buyers apply before contacting sales                           |
| No clear conversion path                              | Demo and sales intent is lost; pipeline starves                                         |
| Weak/absent SEO foundation                            | Invisible in organic search and to AI answer engines                                    |
| Not on the brand domain                               | Brand equity and direct traffic leak away                                               |

## **2.5 The opportunity**

A purpose-built ERP website moves Marmidon from invisible to evaluable. It lets the right buyers find Marmidon, understand the suite, see proof relevant to their sector, and take a clear next step - all while presenting Marmidon at the standard of an established enterprise vendor. This is the difference between a brochure and a working sales asset.

**SECTION 3**

**Business Objectives & Success Metrics**

What good looks like, measured

# **3\. Business Objectives & Success Metrics**

## **3.1 Business objectives (SMART)**

| **ID** | **Objective**                        | **Measure of success**                                                     | **Horizon**      |
| ------ | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| BO-1   | **Establish enterprise credibility** | New site live on <www.marmidon.com>; brand-consistent; 0 placeholder pages | Launch           |
| BO-2   | **Generate qualified demand**        | ≥ 2-4% visitor-to-lead conversion on key pages                             | 3-6 months       |
| BO-3   | **Communicate product depth**        | Every module & sector has a dedicated, complete page                       | Launch           |
| BO-4   | **Be found**                         | Indexed; ranking for target ERP & module/sector terms; AI-answer-ready     | 1-6 months       |
| BO-5   | **Build trust**                      | ≥ 6 case studies / testimonials; client logos; certifications visible      | Launch + ongoing |
| BO-6   | **Be fast & accessible everywhere**  | Core Web Vitals "good"; WCAG 2.2 AA; mobile-first                          | Launch           |
| BO-7   | **Be maintainable by Marmidon**      | Non-developers can edit content via CMS without code                       | Launch           |

## **3.2 Key performance indicators (post-launch)**

| **KPI**                         | **Definition**                        | **Target**                                   |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| Demo / contact conversion rate  | Demo + contact submissions ÷ sessions | 2-4% overall; higher on module/pricing pages |
| Qualified leads / month         | Leads meeting fit criteria            | Baseline in month 1, +20% by month 6         |
| Organic sessions                | Sessions from organic search          | Upward trend month-on-month                  |
| Bounce / engagement rate        | Engaged sessions ÷ total              | Engagement ≥ 55%                             |
| Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)  | Loading performance                   | ≤ 2.5s (75th percentile, mobile)             |
| Interaction to Next Paint (INP) | Responsiveness                        | ≤ 200ms                                      |
| Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)   | Visual stability                      | ≤ 0.1                                        |
| Accessibility conformance       | Automated + manual audit              | WCAG 2.2 AA, 0 critical issues               |

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Instrument before you optimise**

Conversion, KPI and Core Web Vitals targets in this section are only meaningful if the analytics and event tracking specified in TSR-004 are implemented from day one. Do not defer measurement to a later phase.

**SECTION 4**

**Stakeholders & Governance**

Who decides, who is consulted, who is informed

# **4\. Stakeholders & Governance**

## **4.1 Stakeholder register (RACI)**

R = Responsible · A = Accountable · C = Consulted · I = Informed.

| **Stakeholder**        | **Interest**          | **Requirements** | **Design** | **Build** | **Sign-off** |
| ---------------------- | --------------------- | ---------------- | ---------- | --------- | ------------ |
| Project Sponsor        | ROI, brand, budget    | A                | C          | I         | A            |
| Product Owner          | Scope & priorities    | A                | A          | C         | R            |
| Senior BA              | Requirement integrity | R                | C          | C         | C            |
| Web Developer (vendor) | Build & quality       | C                | C          | R/A       | R            |
| Designer               | UX & brand system     | C                | R/A        | C         | C            |
| Content / Marketing    | Messaging & SEO       | C                | C          | R         | C            |
| Sales                  | Lead quality & flow   | C                | I          | I         | C            |
| IT / Security          | Hosting & compliance  | C                | I          | C         | C            |

## **4.2 Decision & change control**

Scope changes after this baseline must be raised as a written change request, assessed for impact on cost, timeline and other requirements, and approved by the Product Owner and Sponsor before work proceeds. The BA maintains the requirements baseline and traceability.

**SECTION 5**

**Target Audiences & Personas**

Designing for committees, not individuals

# **5\. Target Audiences & User Personas**

B2B ERP purchases are made by buying committees, not individuals. The website must let each decision-maker find what they need without wading through content written for someone else. Five primary personas drive the architecture and content.

### **5.1 P1 - The Operations / Business Owner (Economic Buyer)**

| **Attribute**           | **Detail**                                                                      |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Who they are**        | MD, CEO, COO of a mid-sized firm                                                |
| **Key question**        | Will this run my whole business and pay back? Is the vendor credible and local? |
| **What convinces them** | Outcomes, ROI, total cost, proof in my sector, support reliability              |
| **Pages they need**     | Homepage value prop, sector pages, case studies, pricing, demo CTA              |
| **What loses them**     | Jargon, unclear pricing path, no local proof                                    |

### **5.2 P2 - The Finance Decision-Maker**

| **Attribute**           | **Detail**                                                       |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Who they are**        | CFO, Finance Manager, Financial Controller                       |
| **Key question**        | Does it give me control, accuracy and compliance? Audit trail?   |
| **What convinces them** | Financial module depth, compliance, reporting, data integrity    |
| **Pages they need**     | Finance module page, BI page, security & compliance, ROI content |
| **What loses them**     | Vague feature claims, no compliance detail                       |

### **5.3 P3 - The Functional Head**

| **Attribute**           | **Detail**                                                 |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Who they are**        | HR, Procurement, Inventory, Sales, Fleet, Operations leads |
| **Key question**        | Does the module I own actually do my job well?             |
| **What convinces them** | Deep, specific module capability; workflow fit             |
| **Pages they need**     | Individual module pages, feature lists, screenshots, demo  |
| **What loses them**     | Shallow module pages; one-size-fits-all messaging          |

### **5.4 P4 - The IT Evaluator**

| **Attribute**           | **Detail**                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Who they are**        | IT Manager, System Administrator, CTO                              |
| **Key question**        | Architecture, integration, data, hosting, security, support model? |
| **What convinces them** | Technical specs, integrations, deployment, security, SLAs          |
| **Pages they need**     | Technical/architecture content, integrations, security, support    |
| **What loses them**     | Marketing fluff with no technical substance                        |

### **5.5 P5 - The Researcher / Influencer**

| **Attribute**           | **Detail**                                               |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Who they are**        | Analyst, consultant, procurement officer, partner        |
| **Key question**        | Is this shortlist-worthy? How does it compare?           |
| **What convinces them** | Comparisons, resources, documentation, partner programme |
| **Pages they need**     | Resources hub, comparison content, partner page, blog    |
| **What loses them**     | Thin content; nothing to cite or download                |

**BEST PRACTICE · Design implication**

Because no single page can serve all five personas, the architecture must offer parallel paths: a credibility-and-outcome path for P1, deep module/feature paths for P2-P3, a technical substance path for P4, and a resource/comparison path for P5 - all converging on the same demo/contact conversion.

**SECTION 6**

**Competitive Benchmarking**

What the leading ERP websites do - and what we adopt

# **6\. Competitive & Benchmarking Analysis**

Before specifying requirements, we benchmarked how established ERP vendors structure their corporate websites. The patterns below are near-universal in the category and are reflected in the Marmidon requirements and information architecture.

## **6.1 Vendors reviewed**

**Oracle NetSuite** **Odoo** **Acumatica** **SAP (Business One / S/4HANA)** **Sage** **Microsoft Dynamics 365** **Epicor** **Infor** **Zoho** **SYSPRO**

## **6.2 Recurring patterns adopted for Marmidon**

| **Pattern observed across ERP vendors**                                                                         | **Adopt for Marmidon?** | **Where specified**                               |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| Top nav: Products/Modules · Solutions/Industries · Resources · Customers · Pricing · Partners · About · Contact | **Yes**                 | SIA-003 §Navigation                               |
| Mega-menu exposing the full module suite at a glance                                                            | **Yes**                 | FR-NAV, SIA-003                                   |
| Hub-and-spoke: solution hub pages → deep module/feature spokes                                                  | **Yes**                 | SIA-003 §IA model                                 |
| Industry/sector solution pages re-framing the product per buyer                                                 | **Yes**                 | FR-IND                                            |
| Persistent, prominent "Request a demo" / "Contact sales" CTA                                                    | **Yes**                 | FR-NAV, FR-CONV                                   |
| Proof everywhere: case studies, logos, testimonials, numbers                                                    | **Yes**                 | FR-PROOF                                          |
| Resources hub (blog, guides, docs) for SEO & nurture                                                            | **Yes**                 | FR-RES                                            |
| Transparent pricing or clear "how pricing works" path                                                           | **Yes**                 | FR-PRICE                                          |
| Partner / reseller programme page                                                                               | **Yes**                 | FR-PART                                           |
| Heavy free-trial sign-up funnel (Odoo style)                                                                    | **Adapt**               | Demo-led, not trial-led (enterprise sales motion) |

**BENCHMARK · Key takeaway for the developer**

Marmidon's site should feel familiar to anyone who has evaluated NetSuite, Odoo or Acumatica - same structural grammar (Products · Solutions · Industries · Resources · Customers · Pricing · Partners) - but the conversion motion is demo-led and consultative, reflecting an enterprise sale, not a self-serve trial.

**SECTION 7**

**Scope**

What we are building now, later, and not at all

# **7\. Scope Definition**

## **7.1 In scope (Phase 1 - launch)**

- Public marketing website on <www.marmidon.com> with all templates in §8.
- Full module suite (11) and sector (12) pages.
- Resources hub with blog/insights and gated guides.
- Customers / case studies with proof components.
- Pricing or "how pricing works" page with lead capture.
- Demo request, contact and newsletter forms with anti-spam and CRM/email routing.
- CMS allowing non-developers to manage pages, posts, case studies, team and modules.
- SEO foundation, analytics & conversion tracking, accessibility to WCAG 2.2 AA.
- 301 redirect map from old URLs; domain cutover to <www.marmidon.com>.
- Legal pages (privacy, terms, cookie consent).

## **7.2 Phase 2 (post-launch, planned)**

- Client/partner login portal & knowledge base.
- Live chat / chatbot.
- Multi-language / regional variants.
- Interactive ROI / pricing calculator.
- Customer community & events calendar.
- Advanced personalisation by sector.

## **7.3 Out of scope**

- The Marmidon ERP application itself and any in-product functionality.
- Migration of ERP customer data.
- Paid media campaign build and ad creative.
- Email marketing platform configuration beyond form hand-off.

**MUST-HAVE · Scope guardrail**

Anything not listed in §7.1 is not part of the launch build. Requests to add scope must follow §4.2 change control. This protects the launch date and budget.

**SECTION 8**

**Functional Requirements**

Every requirement, identified, prioritised and testable

# **8\. Functional Requirements**

Each requirement has a unique ID, a **MoSCoW priority** (M = Must, S = Should, C = Could, W = Won't-this-phase) and an acceptance condition. IDs are stable references used by the design, content and technical documents and by the test plan in §12.

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · How to read these tables**

M (Must) requirements are launch-blocking - the site cannot go live without them.

S (Should) are high value; defer only with Product Owner approval.

C (Could) are nice-to-have within budget. W (Won't) is explicitly Phase 2.

## **8.1 Global components (apply to every page)**

| **ID**        | **Requirement**                                                                                                               | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                                        |
| ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **FR-NAV-01** | Persistent primary navigation: Products · Solutions/Industries · Resources · Customers · Pricing · Partners · About · Contact | **M**    | All items present, consistent, keyboard-navigable on every page |
| **FR-NAV-02** | Products mega-menu exposing all 11 modules grouped logically                                                                  | **M**    | Hovering/tapping Products reveals all modules with links        |
| **FR-NAV-03** | Persistent primary CTA "Request a Demo" in header                                                                             | **M**    | Visible on all pages incl. mobile; reaches demo form in 1 click |
| **FR-NAV-04** | Secondary CTA "Contact Sales" / phone                                                                                         | **M**    | Reachable from header or sticky element on all pages            |
| **FR-NAV-05** | Sticky header on scroll (condensed)                                                                                           | **S**    | Header remains accessible while scrolling                       |
| **FR-NAV-06** | Mega-footer: modules, sectors, resources, company, legal, social, contact                                                     | **M**    | All groups present and linked; mirrors site map                 |
| **FR-NAV-07** | Breadcrumbs on all pages below level 2                                                                                        | **S**    | Accurate trail reflecting IA on every deep page                 |
| **FR-NAV-08** | Site-wide search                                                                                                              | **S**    | Returns relevant pages, modules, posts, cases                   |
| **FR-NAV-09** | Logo links to homepage from every page                                                                                        | **M**    | Marmidon logo top-left, links home                              |
| **FR-NAV-10** | Cookie consent banner (configurable, compliant)                                                                               | **M**    | Consent captured before non-essential cookies fire              |
| **FR-NAV-11** | Skip-to-content link & visible focus states                                                                                   | **M**    | Keyboard users can skip nav; focus always visible               |

## **8.2 Homepage**

| **ID**         | **Requirement**                                                      | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                                          |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **FR-HOME-01** | Hero with clear value proposition, sub-headline and primary CTA      | **M**    | States what Marmidon is and does without jargon; demo CTA present |
| **FR-HOME-02** | Module suite overview (all 11) with links                            | **M**    | Each module shown and linked to its page                          |
| **FR-HOME-03** | Sector strip (all 12) linking to sector pages                        | **M**    | Each sector shown and linked                                      |
| **FR-HOME-04** | Proof band: client logos / key numbers                               | **M**    | ≥ 1 proof component above the fold area on scroll                 |
| **FR-HOME-05** | Featured case study / testimonial                                    | **S**    | At least one rotating or featured proof story                     |
| **FR-HOME-06** | "Why Marmidon" differentiators (integration, local support, breadth) | **M**    | 3-5 differentiators with supporting copy                          |
| **FR-HOME-07** | Resources teaser (latest insights)                                   | **C**    | Up to 3 latest posts surfaced                                     |
| **FR-HOME-08** | Closing conversion band (demo + contact)                             | **M**    | Strong CTA section before footer                                  |

## **8.3 Product / Module pages (×11)**

Each of the 11 modules has a dedicated page following one template (defined in WDD-002) for consistency and scale.

| **ID**        | **Requirement**                                   | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                            |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| **FR-MOD-01** | Module landing template applied to all 11 modules | **M**    | All 11 module pages exist and use the same template |
| **FR-MOD-02** | Module hero: name, one-line outcome, demo CTA     | **M**    | Present on every module page                        |
| **FR-MOD-03** | Capability/feature list with short descriptions   | **M**    | ≥ 6 capabilities per module, content-managed        |
| **FR-MOD-04** | Benefit/outcome statements (not just features)    | **M**    | Each page frames features as business outcomes      |
| **FR-MOD-05** | Screenshot / visual of the module                 | **S**    | ≥ 1 product visual or annotated placeholder slot    |
| **FR-MOD-06** | "Works with" cross-links to related modules       | **S**    | Links to ≥ 2 related modules (integration story)    |
| **FR-MOD-07** | Relevant sector cross-links                       | **C**    | Links to sectors where module is pivotal            |
| **FR-MOD-08** | Module-level FAQ                                  | **C**    | 3-6 Q&A, content-managed                            |
| **FR-MOD-09** | Per-page demo CTA referencing the module          | **M**    | CTA pre-tags interest = module name on the form     |

## **8.4 Solutions by Industry pages (×12)**

| **ID**        | **Requirement**                                   | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                               |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **FR-IND-01** | Sector landing template applied to all 12 sectors | **M**    | All 12 sector pages exist, same template               |
| **FR-IND-02** | Sector-specific pain points & outcomes            | **M**    | Copy re-framed in the sector's language                |
| **FR-IND-03** | Recommended modules for the sector                | **M**    | Each sector maps to relevant modules with links        |
| **FR-IND-04** | Sector proof (case study / logo) where available  | **S**    | Proof shown when available; graceful fallback when not |
| **FR-IND-05** | Sector-tagged demo CTA                            | **M**    | CTA pre-tags sector on the form                        |

## **8.5 Customers & proof**

| **ID**          | **Requirement**                                                      | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                            |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| **FR-PROOF-01** | Customers / case studies index page                                  | **M**    | Lists all case studies, filterable by sector/module |
| **FR-PROOF-02** | Case study detail template (challenge → solution → result + metrics) | **M**    | Template supports quantified results & quotes       |
| **FR-PROOF-03** | Client logo wall                                                     | **S**    | Logos displayed; content-managed                    |
| **FR-PROOF-04** | Testimonials component (reusable)                                    | **M**    | Embeddable on home, module, sector pages            |
| **FR-PROOF-05** | Trust signals: certifications, years, install base, support stats    | **S**    | Displayed on home/about; content-managed            |

## **8.6 Resources hub**

| **ID**        | **Requirement**                                               | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                         |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **FR-RES-01** | Resources hub landing with content types (blog, guides, FAQs) | **M**    | Hub lists and filters resources                  |
| **FR-RES-02** | Blog/insights index + article template                        | **M**    | Posts created/edited in CMS; categories & tags   |
| **FR-RES-03** | Gated resource (guide/whitepaper) with form capture           | **S**    | Download requires form; lead routed to CRM/email |
| **FR-RES-04** | Related-content & internal linking on articles                | **S**    | Each article links to ≥ 2 related items          |
| **FR-RES-05** | Newsletter subscribe component                                | **S**    | Double opt-in; integrates with email tool        |

## **8.7 Pricing**

| **ID**          | **Requirement**                                     | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                  |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| **FR-PRICE-01** | Pricing or "How pricing works" page                 | **M**    | Explains pricing model & what drives cost |
| **FR-PRICE-02** | Tier/edition comparison or module-based explanation | **S**    | Clear comparison if tiers exist           |
| **FR-PRICE-03** | Primary CTA to quote/demo from pricing              | **M**    | Quote/demo request reachable from pricing |
| **FR-PRICE-04** | Pricing FAQ                                         | **C**    | Common commercial questions answered      |

## **8.8 Partners**

| **ID**         | **Requirement**                   | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**          |
| -------------- | --------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------- |
| **FR-PART-01** | Partner / reseller programme page | **M**    | Explains programme & benefits     |
| **FR-PART-02** | Become-a-partner enquiry form     | **M**    | Submissions routed & acknowledged |
| **FR-PART-03** | Partner directory                 | **C**    | Lists partners if data available  |

## **8.9 Company / About**

| **ID**          | **Requirement**                                | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**          |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------- |
| **FR-ABOUT-01** | About page: mission, story, what Marmidon does | **M**    | Present and brand-consistent      |
| **FR-ABOUT-02** | Leadership / team section                      | **S**    | Content-managed team profiles     |
| **FR-ABOUT-03** | Careers page / open roles                      | **C**    | Roles listed; apply path provided |
| **FR-ABOUT-04** | Contact page: form, phone, email, address, map | **M**    | All channels present; form routed |

## **8.10 Conversion, forms & lead capture**

| **ID**         | **Requirement**                                                                   | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                               |
| -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **FR-CONV-01** | Demo request form (name, company, email, phone, sector, module interest, message) | **M**    | Validates, stores, routes to sales, sends confirmation |
| **FR-CONV-02** | Contact sales form                                                                | **M**    | Routed & acknowledged                                  |
| **FR-CONV-03** | Spam protection (honeypot + CAPTCHA/again)                                        | **M**    | Bot submissions blocked; accessible challenge          |
| **FR-CONV-04** | Lead routing to CRM and/or email + autoresponder                                  | **M**    | Each submission reaches a monitored destination        |
| **FR-CONV-05** | Conversion event tracking on all form submits                                     | **M**    | Analytics records each conversion (see TSR-004)        |
| **FR-CONV-06** | Thank-you / confirmation states with next step                                    | **S**    | User sees confirmation & suggested next action         |
| **FR-CONV-07** | Form field validation & accessible error messaging                                | **M**    | Inline, screen-reader-friendly errors                  |

## **8.11 Content management (CMS)**

| **ID**        | **Requirement**                                                       | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                   |
| ------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| **FR-CMS-01** | Non-developers can create/edit/publish pages & posts                  | **M**    | Marketing edits content without code       |
| **FR-CMS-02** | Structured content types: module, sector, case study, post, team, FAQ | **M**    | Each type editable via its own template    |
| **FR-CMS-03** | Media library with alt-text fields                                    | **M**    | Images managed; alt text enforced          |
| **FR-CMS-04** | Draft / preview / scheduled publish                                   | **S**    | Preview before publish; schedule supported |
| **FR-CMS-05** | Role-based access (admin, editor, author)                             | **S**    | Permissions limit who can publish          |
| **FR-CMS-06** | Reusable components/blocks (CTA, testimonial, proof band)             | **S**    | Editors reuse blocks across pages          |

## **8.12 Cross-cutting functional**

| **ID**      | **Requirement**                                    | **Pri.** | **Acceptance condition**                          |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| **FR-X-01** | Responsive across mobile, tablet, desktop          | **M**    | All templates usable & correct at all breakpoints |
| **FR-X-02** | 404 and error pages with navigation back           | **M**    | Friendly 404 with search & links                  |
| **FR-X-03** | XML sitemap & robots.txt                           | **M**    | Generated & valid (see TSR-004)                   |
| **FR-X-04** | Social share / Open Graph metadata                 | **S**    | Correct preview cards on share                    |
| **FR-X-05** | Cookie/consent management integrates with tracking | **M**    | Tags fire only after consent                      |

**SECTION 9**

**Non-Functional Requirements**

Performance, accessibility, security, SEO - summarised

# **9\. Non-Functional Requirements (Summary)**

Full specifications live in TSR-004. The launch-blocking non-functional targets are summarised here for completeness and acceptance.

| **ID** | **Category**       | **Requirement**                  | **Target / standard**                            |
| ------ | ------------------ | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| NFR-01 | Performance        | Core Web Vitals "good" on mobile | LCP ≤ 2.5s · INP ≤ 200ms · CLS ≤ 0.1             |
| NFR-02 | Accessibility      | Conformance level                | WCAG 2.2 AA, no critical issues                  |
| NFR-03 | Responsive         | Mobile-first across breakpoints  | ≤ 360px to ≥ 1440px verified                     |
| NFR-04 | SEO                | Technical SEO foundation         | Indexable, semantic, structured data             |
| NFR-05 | Security           | Transport & app security         | HTTPS everywhere; OWASP-aware; hardened forms    |
| NFR-06 | Browser support    | Modern evergreen browsers        | Latest 2 versions Chrome/Edge/Firefox/Safari     |
| NFR-07 | Availability       | Hosting uptime                   | ≥ 99.9% target; reputable host/CDN               |
| NFR-08 | Maintainability    | CMS-driven, documented           | Marmidon can maintain without vendor for content |
| NFR-09 | Privacy/compliance | Consent & data handling          | Cookie consent; privacy policy; lawful capture   |
| NFR-10 | Scalability        | Handle growth in pages & traffic | No re-platform needed for foreseeable growth     |

**SECTION 10**

**Key User Journeys**

The paths that must work end to end

# **10\. Key User Journeys**

| **Journey**                                     | **Path**                                                                                   | **Successful outcome**                     |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ |
| **J1 - Owner evaluates fit (P1)**               | Homepage → "Why Marmidon" → relevant Sector page → Case study → Request a Demo             | Demo request submitted, sector pre-tagged  |
| **J2 - Functional head assesses a module (P3)** | Search/Products menu → Module page → capabilities → related modules → Demo (module-tagged) | Module-tagged demo request                 |
| **J3 - Finance buyer checks compliance (P2)**   | Finance module → BI/reporting → Security/compliance content → Contact sales                | Contact submitted with finance interest    |
| **J4 - IT evaluator vets technically (P4)**     | About/architecture → integrations → security → support model → Contact                     | Technical contact submitted                |
| **J5 - Researcher gathers evidence (P5)**       | Resources hub → guide (gated) → blog → Customers → newsletter/demo                         | Lead captured via gated content/newsletter |
| **J6 - Returning warm lead converts**           | Direct/branded search → Pricing → "how pricing works" → Request quote/demo                 | Quote/demo request submitted               |

**BEST PRACTICE · Every journey ends in conversion**

Each primary journey terminates in a demo, contact, quote or nurture capture. If any journey reaches a dead end without a next step, the architecture has failed and must be corrected before launch.

**SECTION 11**

**Assumptions, Dependencies, Constraints & Risks**

# **11\. Assumptions, Dependencies, Constraints & Risks**

## **11.1 Assumptions**

- Marmidon will provide approved copy, brand assets, client logos and case-study material, or approve placeholders.
- Approved high-resolution logo and brand colour (#3588E4) are final.
- Marmidon will provide access to the <www.marmidon.com> domain and DNS.
- A CRM or destination inbox exists for lead routing.

## **11.2 Dependencies**

- Domain & DNS control for cutover.
- Content & assets availability gates page completion.
- Third-party services: analytics, email/CRM, CAPTCHA, hosting/CDN.
- Legal review of privacy/terms/cookie copy.

## **11.3 Constraints**

- Must launch on <www.marmidon.com> and retire the placeholder sub-domain.
- Must remain maintainable by non-developers post-launch.
- Must meet WCAG 2.2 AA and Core Web Vitals at launch.

## **11.4 Risk register**

| **ID** | **Risk**                                   | **Likelihood** | **Impact** | **Mitigation**                                                                |
| ------ | ------------------------------------------ | -------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| R1     | Content/assets delayed                     | High           | High       | Lock content deadlines; use approved placeholders; CMS lets content land late |
| R2     | Scope creep                                | Medium         | High       | §4.2 change control; baseline this PRD                                        |
| R3     | SEO regression at domain cutover           | Medium         | High       | 301 redirect map (SIA-003); submit sitemap; monitor                           |
| R4     | Performance budget breached by heavy media | Medium         | Medium     | Performance budget in TSR-004; image optimisation                             |
| R5     | Accessibility gaps found late              | Medium         | Medium     | Build to WCAG 2.2 AA from start; audit before launch                          |
| R6     | Lead routing misconfigured                 | Low            | High       | Test every form end-to-end in UAT (§12)                                       |

**SECTION 12**

**Acceptance Criteria & UAT**

How we agree the website is done

# **12\. Acceptance Criteria & UAT Approach**

The website is accepted when all Must requirements pass, Should requirements are met or formally deferred, and the launch gates below are green.

## **12.1 Launch acceptance gates**

| **Gate**          | **Criteria**                                                             | **Verified by**     |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------- |
| **Functional**    | All FR "M" requirements pass; all 11 module & 12 sector pages complete   | BA + Product Owner  |
| **Content**       | No placeholder/lorem text on published pages; all CTAs work              | Content lead        |
| **Forms & leads** | Every form validated, spam-protected, and routed; confirmations sent     | BA + Sales          |
| **Performance**   | Core Web Vitals "good" on key templates (mobile)                         | Developer + BA      |
| **Accessibility** | WCAG 2.2 AA; 0 critical automated/manual issues                          | Developer + BA      |
| **SEO**           | Indexable, metadata complete, sitemap & robots valid, redirects in place | Content + Developer |
| **Responsive**    | Verified mobile/tablet/desktop                                           | QA                  |
| **Analytics**     | Tracking & conversion events firing (TSR-004)                            | Developer + BA      |
| **Legal**         | Privacy, terms, cookie consent live & approved                           | Sponsor             |
| **Cutover**       | Live on <www.marmidon.com>; placeholder retired; SSL valid               | Developer           |

## **12.2 Test approach**

- Requirement-by-requirement verification using FR IDs as test cases.
- Cross-browser & cross-device testing matrix (TSR-004).
- End-to-end form & lead-routing tests with real destinations.
- Automated + manual accessibility audit.
- Performance audit (Lighthouse/field data) on key templates.
- Stakeholder UAT sign-off against §12.1 before go-live.

**SECTION A**

**Appendices**

Glossary, traceability and reference

# **Appendix A. Glossary**

| **Term**        | **Meaning**                                                            |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| ERP             | Enterprise Resource Planning - integrated business management software |
| PRD/BRD/FRD     | Product / Business / Functional Requirements Document                  |
| MoSCoW          | Must / Should / Could / Won't prioritisation                           |
| Core Web Vitals | Google's LCP/INP/CLS performance metrics                               |
| WCAG 2.2 AA     | Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, conformance level AA             |
| CMS             | Content Management System                                              |
| IA              | Information Architecture                                               |
| CTA             | Call To Action                                                         |
| Hub-and-spoke   | Broad hub pages linking to deep spoke pages                            |
| UAT             | User Acceptance Testing                                                |

# **Appendix B. Requirements traceability (summary)**

Each requirement group traces to one or more business objectives and to the companion document that elaborates it, ensuring nothing is built without a reason and nothing required is missed.

| **Requirement group** | **Serves objective** | **Elaborated in**         |
| --------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------- |
| FR-NAV / global       | BO-1, BO-2           | SIA-003, WDD-002          |
| FR-HOME               | BO-1, BO-2, BO-5     | WDD-002, CPM-005          |
| FR-MOD (modules)      | BO-3                 | WDD-002, CPM-005, SIA-003 |
| FR-IND (sectors)      | BO-3, BO-2           | WDD-002, CPM-005, SIA-003 |
| FR-PROOF              | BO-5                 | CPM-005, WDD-002          |
| FR-RES                | BO-4, BO-2           | CPM-005, TSR-004          |
| FR-CONV               | BO-2                 | TSR-004, WDD-002          |
| FR-CMS                | BO-7                 | TSR-004                   |
| NFR-\*                | BO-4, BO-6           | TSR-004                   |

# **Appendix C. Master taxonomies**

### **Modules (11)**

**M1 Financials** **M2 HR & Payroll** **M3 Inventory** **M4 Procurement** **M5 Sales & CRM** **M6 Operations** **M7 Fleet** **M8 Manufacturing** **M9 POS & Retail** **M10 BI & Dashboards** **M11 Projects**

### **Sectors (12)**

**Manufacturing** **Production** **Wholesale** **Retail** **Distribution** **Professional Services** **Education** **Healthcare** **Hospitality** **Non-Profit** **Construction** **Media & Publishing**

**- End of Document MARM-WEB-PRD-001 -**
